title: anaxagorea borneensis
intro: Anaxagorea borneensis, also known as the Borneo camphor tree, is a tropical plant species native to Borneo, belonging to the Annonaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of limonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Anaxagorea borneensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infectious diseases. An herbal preparation of the plant's bark can be made into a tea or infused oil, while its seeds can be used to extract a camphor-like essential oil, highlighting its versatility in traditional medicine.
